rmk:
Identification not 100% sure, but "Eumops sp." is no option on Xeno-Canto yet. Guess is based on the following fact about Eumops trumbulli: "Larger member of the genus". Often larger bats go lower in frequency and the call frequency is too low for E. auripendulus, E. maurus and E. glaucinus. The species E. perotis and E. dabbenei do not appear in this area.

Identified by Sarah Mahie and Boaz van Die while using the following sources:

- [Arias-Aguilar, Adriana & Hintze, Frederico & Aguiar, Ludmilla & Rufray, Vincent & Bernard, Enrico & Ramos Pereira, Maria João. (2018). Who’s calling? Acoustic identification of Brazilian bats. Mammal Research. 63. 10.1007/s13364-018-0367-z.](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/324708861_Who's_calling_Acoustic_identification_of_Brazilian_bats)
- [Distrubution map of the species.](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Colombian_bonneted_bat)
